subject: Product Management
title: Correlation vs Causation
description: A quick win: understand Correlation vs Causation.

hook.text: Ice cream sales and drowning deaths both rise in summer. Ice cream doesn't cause drowning.

buildup.text: Correlation means two things move together. Causation means one thing causes the other. Product teams confuse them constantly.

discovery.text: 'Users who complete onboarding retain better.' But is it because onboarding helps, or because motivated users do both?

twist.text: The only way to prove causation is experimentation: randomly assign users to groups and measure the difference.

climax.text: Teams that act on correlations without testing causation build features that move metrics without actually helping users.

quiz.question: Why is confusing correlation with causation dangerous for product teams?
options: They might build features that move metrics without actually helping users
Correlation and causation always mean the same thing
Only scientists need to worry about this distinction
